page.content: The following data table presents a comprehensive analysis of the interaction between various water treatment types and their effects on health outcomes, as indicated by key metrics such as the Median Case Count (n) and Median Attack Rate (n). Understanding the relationship between these variables is critical for developing effective public health interventions, particularly in regions affected by waterborne diseases. The table categorizes data based on the specific water treatment types employed, allowing for a comparative analysis of their efficacy. For instance, the Median Case Count (n) reflects the number of reported cases associated with each treatment type, while the Median Attack Rate (n) provides insight into the incidence of disease within the population. Additionally, the table includes metrics on the structural parameters of the treatments, such as Nodes, Elements, and Max. Elem. Vol. (mm³), which are essential for evaluating the operational effectiveness of each treatment system. This data will be instrumental in informing policy decisions and guiding future research in the field of water treatment and public health.

page.html:
Water Treatment TypeMedian Case Count (n)Median Attack Rate (n)
Untreated31 (704)25.6% (70)
Disinfection-Only94 (271)16.7% (74)
Disinfection-and-Filtration108 (117)28.2% (32)
Other Treatment 1207 (72)5.1% (21)
Unknown Treatment53 (355)13.9% (17)
